full_text_rationale: Full text describes CodeDistiller generating a vetted reusable code-example library from repositories for scientific coding agents, with automatic/domain-expert evaluation and downstream agent evaluation.
titulo: CodeDistiller: Automatically Generating Code Libraries for Scientific Coding Agents
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 004; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Peter A. Jansen, Samiah Hassan, Pragnya Narasimha
formato_armazenamento: Vetted library of executable, debugged scientific code examples distilled from GitHub repositories, including example code, requirements, suitability descriptions, and evidence of successful execution.
framework_agente: CodeDistiller and downstream Code-RAG / CodeScientist-style automated scientific discovery agent
llm_utilizado: GPT-OSS-120B, GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4.5, smaller classifier variants such as GPT-5-mini and Claude Haiku 4.5
benchmarks_utilizados: 250 materials-science GitHub repositories; 50-repository domain-expert validation subset; 60 downstream materials-science tasks from 12 repositories
metricas_desempenho: Automatic successful completion; manual code-execution success; manual repository-functionality and correct-functionality judgments; runtime; debug iterations; API cost; A/B preference proportions for accuracy, completeness, and scientific soundness
baseline_comparado: Different CodeDistiller base models; downstream CodeScientist with generic materials-science examples versus CodeDistiller-generated examples.
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Library is generated from a collection of repository examples; growth bound or update policy is not reported.
custo_por_tarefa: Successful-case average cost: GPT-OSS-120B $0.09, GPT-5 $0.70, Claude 4.5 $1.71; downstream runs allow up to $5 of LLM-associated costs per run.
resultado_principal: CodeDistiller converts scientific GitHub repositories into reusable code examples. Domain-expert validation reports correct functionality for 25.9% with GPT-OSS-120B, 60.5% with GPT-5, and 74.1% with Claude 4.5; downstream A/B tests prefer CodeDistiller-augmented outputs in nearly half of cases across accuracy, completeness, and soundness.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Downstream A/B preference proportions favor CodeDistiller over the generic-example baseline for accuracy (0.50 vs 0.26), completeness (0.46 vs 0.16), and soundness (0.50 vs 0.30), with ties making up the remainder.
custo_vs_baseline: Claude-generated examples cost 19 times more on average than GPT-OSS examples; no downstream cost reduction claim is reported.
limitacoes: LLM-as-judge overestimates success, especially for GPT-OSS-120B; unsuccessful examples cost more because debugging reaches hard iteration limits; domain-expert evaluation is limited to a subsample and downstream tasks are materials-science specific.
ameacas_validade: Materials-science domain focus, repository filtering, domain-expert subsampling, judge-model preference evaluation, run budget, and debug-iteration caps may affect external validity.
